SL يها ناه = | Nhs 2 שר - - = = Fe بج "لب Sm So a Bring Warfighter Capabilities To The Tactical Edge Timely, Actionable Information to Enhance Survivability, Lethality, and Mission Success If you think about the challenge we have in AI, in amalgamating data from sensors and sharing data, what it means to take processing power and move that processing requirement to the tactical edge, without a cloud connection, Leidos, in collaboration with our military partners, created our Edge to Cloud (E2C) solution to enable cloud capabilities at the tactical edge through container-based virtualization. [PAGE 2 TEXT]: E2C not only connects sensors, software, and services, but also provides a coherent framework for data management, information distribution, and analytic exploitation. E2C replaces the legacy approach of ex-post-facto integration of tactical devices by bringing the power of cloud computing to the point of collection, aggregation, and analysis through the use of commodity hardware. By deploying E2C Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using the Leidos DevSecOps process, E2C enables the standardization of infrastructure deployment models and rapid instantiation of those models to any level of the E2C ecosystem. The E2C approach features containerized software deployment to quickly and autonomously propagate upgrades, data, models, and software deployments across the ecosystem. E2C also enables an ever-expanding network of devices and sensors to collect, process, aggregate, and communicate information Additionally, system data can be processed and analyzed in near real-time, enhancing situational awareness. But E2C goes far beyond “cloud at the edge”, with several significant advances in processes and technologies that can immediately augment any organization's current and future missions. E2C delivers: OUR CAPABILITIES * A cloud computing ecosystem that enables collection, aggregation, and * System engineering & integration and dissemination of data from producers to users, operators, and decision-makers to life cycle support of mission-critical enable efficient, effective decisions and actions applications for a broad spectrum of government customers. * A software defined ecosystem, employing an “Everything as Code” approach for infrastructure, configuration, security, and applications (This means E2C can provision = * Rapid and agile research, a new environment to host a tenant, mission app owner, or project within minutes) development, integration, test, evaluation and transition capabilities. * Amodel for how edge devices (mobile compute, smart phones, edge sensors, wearables, Internet of Things devices) can be integrated to increase the speed and * Deep domain knowledge of soldier- accuracy of data correlation to support decision making at the edge. borne information and communication systems, gained from decades of * An extensive palette of DevSecOps capabilities across the entire compute experience supporting a long legacy ecosystem, enabling a “Software Factory in a Box", and allowing the deployment of a of Army dismounted soldier situation DevSecOps pipeline to any layer or node in the ecosystem in minutes. awareness programs. * Cloud-based development tools in which operators can develop and deploy from + Leading-edge sensor application anywhere. We augmented this capability with templates, configuration, tradecraft, expertise and R&D programs and scripts in the ecosystem. * Extensive data analytics, exploitation, * Automated deployment of containerized mission software, models, applications, and Al experience to locate, integrate, and microservices to any layer of the ecosystem (tactical edge, on premise, and process relevant information and federated, enterprise cloud, commercial cloud, or hybrid). transform it into prioritized actionable * A hardware, cloud, and vendor-interoperable design: E2C can be extended information to Microsoft Azure, Google Cloud Platform, and other cloud service providers, Expertise in cybersecurity and virtualized on-premise of hybrid models information assurance for various U.S. * Management and orchestration of all ecosystem nodes (and all containerized Government Agencies, Intelligence applications/services therein) from any other authorized and provisioned node Community (IC) organizations, and commercial customers. WHY PARTNER WITH LEIDOS Leidos approached this challenge through a combination of Internal Research and Development funds and a Cooperative Research and Development Agreement with the U.S. Army. We teamed with partners such as Amazon Web Services (AWS), HashiCorp, Rancher Federal, GitLab, Telos and other technology leaders to build an ecosystem that would extend the cloud to the tactical edge. Our team’s prior service members brought an operational focus to develop the underlying systems, devices, and connections that power cloud at the edge and to meet the challenges posed by near-peer and Great Power threats. And we adopted recent innovations in systems composition, deployment, management, and connectivity that will enable rapid provisioning, deployment, and sustainment across the enterprise and the larger partner community. FOR MORE INFORMATION leidos.com/defense 2024 LEIDOS. ALL RIGHTS RESERVED. THE INFORMATION IN THIS DOCUMENT IS PROPRIETARY ‏סד‎ LEIDOS. E2C replaces the legacy approach of ex-post-facto integration of tactical devices by bringing the power of cloud computing to the point of collection, aggregation, and analysis through the use of commodity hardware. By deploying E2C Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using the Leidos DevSecOps process, E2C enables the standardization of infrastructure deployment models and rapid instantiation of those models to any level of the E2C ecosystem. The E2C approach features containerized software deployment to quickly and autonomously propagate upgrades, data, models, and software deployments across the ecosystem. E2C also enables an ever-expanding network of devices and sensors to collect, process, aggregate, and communicate information. Additionally, system data can be processed and analyzed in near real-time, enhancing situational awareness. But E2C goes far beyond "cloud at the edge", with several significant advances in processes and technologies that can immediately augment any organization's current and future missions.
